response:
to find the denominator we must store the count of factors of two and five in the factorial.
***
the number of factors of five in one hundred factorial is stored by summing the quotients of division.
***
we store the result of dividing one hundred by five which gives us twenty as the first term.
***
next we store the result of dividing twenty by five which gives us four as the second term.
***
the total count of factors of five is twenty plus four which equals twenty four and we store this value.
***
the number of factors of two in one hundred factorial is much larger than the count of factors of five.
***
when we divide by ten to the power of fifty we remove fifty pairs of two and five from the store.
***
since there are only twenty four fives we cannot remove fifty pairs so the fraction is not an integer.
***
the remaining denominator will be ten raised to the power of the difference between fifty and twenty four.
***
thus the final answer is ten to the power of twenty six which we now store as the irreducible denominator.
